title: Rice straw tissue preparation protocol for reproducible electron microscopy imaging and analysis
author: Mohamadiaza, Mahta
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Common problems in biological sample processing for the scanning electron microscope (SEM) include cell collapse and destruction. To overcome challenges surrounding the SEM micrograph preparation, dried rice stem was used to develop a specific protocol to process dried plant samples. Dried stems were rehydrated with a glycerol solution and fixed by formalin-acetic-alcohol to avoid cell wall collapse or organ distortion. The protocol detailed here is the first study on preparing SEM images of dried plant tissue.      The protocol provides a low-cost route to obtain good-quality micrographs and to help reconstruct growth processes and study plant cell wall features.

The protocol provides a low-cost route to obtain good-quality micrographs and to help reconstruct growth processes and study plant cell wall features.
